tokenFamilies.label: METAR (routine)
tokenFamilies.decode: Routine hourly observation, issued near H+55.

references.detail: Chapter 3 - Surface Aviation Weather Observations (METAR / SPECI)
7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: SPECI (special / off-cycle)
tokenFamilies.decode: Off-cycle observation issued when a reportable change occurs between routine reports.

references.detail: Chapter 3 - Surface Aviation Weather Observations (METAR / SPECI)
7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: AUTO (automated station)
tokenFamilies.decode: Automated station with no human observer; pair with RMK AO1 / AO2 to know if precipitation can be discriminated.

references.detail: Chapter 3, modifier group + RMK station type
Chapter 3 - Surface Aviation Weather Observations (METAR / SPECI)
7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: COR (corrected)
tokenFamilies.decode: The previous observation was wrong; this report supersedes it.

references.detail: Chapter 3 - Surface Aviation Weather Observations (METAR / SPECI)
7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: Calm
tokenFamilies.decode: Direction zero, speed zero. Encoded `00000KT`.

references.detail: Chapter 3 - Surface Aviation Weather Observations (METAR / SPECI)
7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: Steady direction + speed
tokenFamilies.decode: Three-digit direction (degrees true) + two-digit speed (knots) + `KT`.

references.detail: Chapter 3 - Surface Aviation Weather Observations (METAR / SPECI)
7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: Gust group
tokenFamilies.decode: G suffix + two-digit gust value when peak in last 10 min exceeds the 2-min mean by 10+ KT.

references.detail: Chapter 3 - Surface Aviation Weather Observations (METAR / SPECI)
7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: Variable direction (VRB) - low wind
tokenFamilies.decode: Speed below 6 KT and direction cannot be resolved. Encoded `VRB` + two-digit speed.

references.detail: Chapter 3 - Surface Aviation Weather Observations (METAR / SPECI)
7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: Variable range (nnnVnnn)
tokenFamilies.decode: Primary direction varies > 60 deg with speed > 6 KT. Separate group `dirVdir` follows the wind group.

references.detail: Chapter 3 - Surface Aviation Weather Observations (METAR / SPECI)
7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: Above 10 SM
tokenFamilies.decode: Always encoded `10SM`; US METARs never report higher.

references.detail: Chapter 3 - Surface Aviation Weather Observations (METAR / SPECI)
7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: Whole-mile
tokenFamilies.decode: Statute miles, integer, with `SM` suffix.

references.detail: Chapter 3 - Surface Aviation Weather Observations (METAR / SPECI)
7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: Fractional - below 3 SM
tokenFamilies.decode: Pure fractions or mixed whole-plus-fraction with mandatory space. `1 1/2SM`, `3/4SM`, `1/4SM`.

references.detail: Chapter 3 - Surface Aviation Weather Observations (METAR / SPECI)
7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: Less-than (M) prefix
tokenFamilies.decode: The `M` prefix means "less than." `M1/4SM` = visibility less than 1/4 SM (the lowest reportable prevailing visibility).

references.detail: Chapter 3 - Surface Aviation Weather Observations (METAR / SPECI)
7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: Light intensity (-)
tokenFamilies.decode: The `-` prefix marks light intensity. Common with `-RA`, `-SN`, `-SHSN`, `-FZRA`.

references.detail: Chapter 3 - Surface Aviation Weather Observations (METAR / SPECI)
7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: Moderate intensity (no prefix)
tokenFamilies.decode: Bare phenomenon = moderate intensity. `RA`, `TSRA`, `SN`.

references.detail: Chapter 3 - Surface Aviation Weather Observations (METAR / SPECI)
7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: Heavy intensity (+)
tokenFamilies.decode: The `+` prefix marks heavy intensity. `+TSRA`, `+SN`, `+FC`.

references.detail: Chapter 3 - Surface Aviation Weather Observations (METAR / SPECI)
7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: Vicinity (VC)
tokenFamilies.decode: The `VC` prefix = phenomenon observed within 5-10 SM of the station but not at the station.

references.detail: Chapter 3 - Surface Aviation Weather Observations (METAR / SPECI)
7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: Descriptor + phenomenon combos
tokenFamilies.decode: Descriptors `TS`, `SH`, `FZ`, `BL`, `DR`, `MI`, `BC`, `PR` compose with the phenomenon list. Common combos memorized on sight (`+TSRA`, `FZRA`, `+FC`, `MIFG`).

references.detail: Chapter 3, weather group descriptor + phenomenon table
Chapter 3 - Surface Aviation Weather Observations (METAR / SPECI)
7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: BR vs FG (mist vs fog boundary)
tokenFamilies.decode: BR (mist) = visibility 5/8 to 7 SM. FG (fog) = visibility under 5/8 SM. The boundary matters for approach minima.

references.detail: Chapter 3, mist vs fog reporting threshold
Chapter 3 - Surface Aviation Weather Observations (METAR / SPECI)
7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: Clear variants (SKC / CLR)
tokenFamilies.decode: SKC = human-observer clear sky. CLR = automated station reporting no clouds below 12,000 ft. NSC = international "no significant cloud."

references.detail: Chapter 3 - Surface Aviation Weather Observations (METAR / SPECI)
7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: Single-layer
tokenFamilies.decode: One cover + height pair. Cover ascending; height in hundreds of feet AGL.

references.detail: Chapter 3 - Surface Aviation Weather Observations (METAR / SPECI)
7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: Multi-layer
tokenFamilies.decode: Multiple cover + height pairs, ascending. The ceiling is the lowest BKN or OVC layer.

references.detail: Chapter 3 - Surface Aviation Weather Observations (METAR / SPECI)
7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: Cumulonimbus (CB) suffix
tokenFamilies.decode: A `CB` suffix after the layer height marks an active thunderstorm cell. Almost always paired with TS in the weather group.

references.detail: Chapter 3 - Surface Aviation Weather Observations (METAR / SPECI)
7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: Towering cumulus (TCU) suffix
tokenFamilies.decode: A `TCU` suffix after the layer height marks a cell building toward thunderstorm but not yet producing lightning.

references.detail: Chapter 3 - Surface Aviation Weather Observations (METAR / SPECI)
7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: Vertical visibility (VV)
tokenFamilies.decode: Substitutes for the sky-condition group when the sky is obscured (dense fog, heavy snow, volcanic ash). `VV002` = vertical visibility 200 ft.

references.detail: Chapter 3 - Surface Aviation Weather Observations (METAR / SPECI)
7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: Both positive
tokenFamilies.decode: Temp / dew point both > 0 deg C. Slash-separated, no M prefix.

references.detail: Chapter 3 - Surface Aviation Weather Observations (METAR / SPECI)
7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: Both negative
tokenFamilies.decode: Both endpoints get the M prefix. `M08/M14` = -8 / -14 deg C.

references.detail: Chapter 3 - Surface Aviation Weather Observations (METAR / SPECI)
7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: Cross-zero (one side +, one side -)
tokenFamilies.decode: Temperature positive, dew point negative (or vice versa). Common around frontal passages.

references.detail: Chapter 3 - Surface Aviation Weather Observations (METAR / SPECI)
7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: Narrow spread (saturation)
tokenFamilies.decode: Temp - dew point of 1-2 deg C. Mist territory; a half-degree of cooling produces fog.

references.detail: Chapter 3, operational reading of the temp / dew spread
Chapter 3 - Surface Aviation Weather Observations (METAR / SPECI)
7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: Standard / near-standard
tokenFamilies.decode: A + four digits = inHg x 100. A2992 = 29.92 inHg (standard).

references.detail: Chapter 3 - Surface Aviation Weather Observations (METAR / SPECI)
7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: Low (deepening low / trough)
tokenFamilies.decode: Sub-standard altimeter signals approaching or deepening low pressure.

references.detail: Chapter 3 - Surface Aviation Weather Observations (METAR / SPECI)
7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: High (strong surface high)
tokenFamilies.decode: Above-standard altimeter signals a building or established surface high.

references.detail: Chapter 3 - Surface Aviation Weather Observations (METAR / SPECI)
7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: International Q-group (hectopascals)
tokenFamilies.decode: A `Q` group + four digits = pressure in hectopascals (e.g., Q1023 = 1023 hPa).

references.detail: Chapter 3 - Surface Aviation Weather Observations (METAR / SPECI)
7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: CAVOK (international)
tokenFamilies.decode: Single token replacing visibility + weather + sky-condition when visibility >= 10 km, no cloud below 5000 ft (or MSA), no significant weather.

references.detail: Chapter 3 - Surface Aviation Weather Observations (METAR / SPECI)
7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

examples.synoptic: ORD under a surface high in cool dry air; standard altimeter, calm-confidence morning.

references.detail: Chapter 3 - Surface Aviation Weather Observations (METAR / SPECI)
7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

examples.synoptic: Paris CDG with 9999 visibility group (international form for >= 10 km) and Q-pressure.

references.detail: Chapter 3 - Surface Aviation Weather Observations (METAR / SPECI)
7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: Routine TAF
tokenFamilies.decode: Four-times-daily issuance at 00 / 06 / 12 / 18 Z with a 24-30 hour validity window.

references.detail: 7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: Amended TAF (AMD)
tokenFamilies.decode: Off-cycle issuance superseding the prior TAF because conditions diverged enough to require a new forecast.

references.detail: 7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: Corrected TAF (COR)
tokenFamilies.decode: The prior TAF was wrong (transcription / missed group). The COR replaces it, validity unchanged.

references.detail: 7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: DDHH/DDHH validity window
tokenFamilies.decode: Day + hour, twice. First pair = start; second pair = end. 24-30 hour window at major airports.

references.detail: 7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: Initial body (prevailing) - VFR
tokenFamilies.decode: The body following the validity range describes the prevailing forecast at window start. Holds until first change group.

references.detail: 7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: FM (from) - definite shift
tokenFamilies.decode: FM + DDhhmm marks a definite lasting change. The new body replaces the prevailing until the next FM (or validity end).

references.detail: 7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: TEMPO - transient excursion
tokenFamilies.decode: TEMPO + DDHH/DDHH marks brief excursions (< 1 hour, less than half the period). The prevailing returns when the window ends.

references.detail: 7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: PROB30 - 30% probability
tokenFamilies.decode: PROB30 + DDHH/DDHH marks a 30-50% chance of the named condition during the window. Reserved for significant weather where confidence is below the FM threshold.

references.detail: 7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: PROB40 - 40% probability
tokenFamilies.decode: PROB40 + DDHH/DDHH. Same shape as PROB30 with a 40-50% confidence range.

references.detail: 7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: BECMG - becoming
tokenFamilies.decode: BECMG + DDHH/DDHH marks a gradual transition. By window end the new condition is prevailing. Used for slow changes (gradual fronts / clearing).

references.detail: 7-1-29 - Key to Aerodrome Forecast (TAF) and Aviation Routine Weather Report (METAR)

tokenFamilies.label: UA (routine)
tokenFamilies.decode: Routine pilot report. Not safety-critical; informational for the next aircraft along that track.

tokenFamilies.label: UUA (urgent)
tokenFamilies.decode: Urgent pilot report. Severe icing / turbulence / hail / shear / volcanic ash / tornadoes / waterspouts.

tokenFamilies.label: Bare station ICAO
tokenFamilies.decode: /OV STATION - report at the station itself.

tokenFamilies.label: Station + radial + distance
tokenFamilies.decode: /OV STA + 3-digit radial + 2-3 digit distance NM. Position relative to a known fix.

tokenFamilies.label: 4-letter ICAO + radial + distance
tokenFamilies.decode: /OV KSTA + radial + distance. Less common; Canadian / European cross-border reports.

tokenFamilies.label: HHMM (Z)
tokenFamilies.decode: Four-digit UTC time. No Z suffix in the encoded form.

tokenFamilies.label: Altitude (FL or DURC/DURD)
tokenFamilies.decode: FLnnn in hundreds of feet. DURC = during climb; DURD = during descent (vertical slice).

tokenFamilies.label: Aircraft type
tokenFamilies.decode: 4-letter ICAO aircraft code. Type matters because turbulence / icing severity is relative to airframe.

tokenFamilies.label: Sky single layer
tokenFamilies.decode: One cover + base-top pair. /SK BKN090 = broken at 9,000 ft, no top reported.

tokenFamilies.label: Sky multi-layer with tops
tokenFamilies.decode: Multiple cover + base-top pairs. /SK SCT040-060 BKN090 = scattered 4,000-6,000, broken 9,000 base.

tokenFamilies.label: Temperature (Celsius)
tokenFamilies.decode: /TA + integer C. M (or -) prefix for negative. /TA M02 = -2 C.

tokenFamilies.label: Wind (direction + speed)
tokenFamilies.decode: /WV 3-digit dir + 2-3 digit speed + optional KT. Direction is degrees true.

tokenFamilies.label: Turbulence - LGT
tokenFamilies.decode: Light turbulence. Brief erratic changes in altitude / attitude / less than 1/3 G excursions.

tokenFamilies.label: Turbulence - MOD
tokenFamilies.decode: Moderate turbulence. Loose objects rattle; 1/3 to 1 G excursions; difficulty walking.

tokenFamilies.label: Turbulence - SEV (UUA)
tokenFamilies.decode: Severe turbulence. Aircraft thrown violently about; momentary loss of control; > 1 G excursions. UUA.

tokenFamilies.label: Turbulence - EXTM (UUA)
tokenFamilies.decode: Extreme turbulence. Aircraft practically impossible to control; structural damage likely. UUA.

tokenFamilies.label: Icing - TRC (trace)
tokenFamilies.decode: Trace icing. Ice perceptible; rate of accumulation slightly greater than sublimation. Not hazardous unless prolonged.

tokenFamilies.label: Icing - LGT
tokenFamilies.decode: Light icing. May create problem if flight in icing prolonged > 1 hr; pilot can manage with deicing / heat / altitude change.

tokenFamilies.label: Icing - MOD
tokenFamilies.decode: Moderate icing. Encounters become potentially hazardous; even short encounters need course / altitude change.

tokenFamilies.label: Icing - SEV (UUA)
tokenFamilies.decode: Severe icing. Deicing / anti-icing fails to control; immediate diversion is necessary. UUA.

tokenFamilies.label: Icing type (RIME / CLR / MX)
tokenFamilies.decode: RIME = white opaque (small droplets, slow accretion). CLR = clear (large droplets, fast). MX = mixed.

examples.synoptic: GA aircraft west of ORD reporting broken cumulus base 5,000 ft.
737 south of SFO at 5,000 ft; westerly 45-kt wind verifying the FB forecast.

examples.raw: KMIA UA /OV MIA270030 /TM 1600 /FL090 /TP C402 /SK SCT040-060 BKN090
examples.synoptic: C402 west of MIA reporting scattered cumulus tops at 6,000 with broken layer above.

examples.raw: KGRR UUA /OV GRR /TM 1530 /FL060 /TP PA28 /TA M04 /IC MOD RIME 050-070
examples.synoptic: PA-28 in moderate rime ice between 5,000 and 7,000 ft west of Lake Michigan.
CRJ-700 south of Denver in severe turbulence between FL200 and FL240.

examples.raw: KORD UUA /OV ORD180040 /TM 1820 /FL090 /TP B738 /TA M08 /IC SEV CLR 080-100
examples.synoptic: 737 south of ORD with severe clear ice 8,000-10,000 ft; deep supercooled drizzle layer.
GA aircraft east of CLE; minimal block coverage (no weather group).
PA-28 southwest of BOS at 6,000; broken stratus base 5,000.
CRJ east of Toronto Pearson; overcast base 8,000 with cruise at 9,000.

examples.raw: KMSP UA /OV MSP /TM 1600 /FL070 /TP C172 /IC TRC RIME 060-080
examples.synoptic: C172 at MSP reporting trace rime ice 6,000-8,000; non-known-ice airframe at decision point.
A320 at FL340 over JFK reporting light turbulence; informational only.

examples.raw: KORD UA /OV ORD /TM 1900 /FL350 /TP B752 /TB MOD 300-360 /RM CONTINUOUS LIGHT CHOP
examples.synoptic: 757 at FL350 over ORD reporting moderate turbulence with continuous light chop in remarks.

examples.raw: KORD UA /OV ORD090020 /TM 1745 /FL080 /TP C172 /SK BKN050-080 /TA M02 /TB MOD
examples.synoptic: 172 east of ORD; tops 8,000 in moderate turbulence with -2 C.
737 climbing through OVC020 with tops at 5,000 ft south of ORD.
PA-28 east of CLE; scattered base 3,500 ft, VFR-friendly cumulus.
C402 west of MIA at 9,000 ft; single broken layer at cruise altitude.
A320 at FL340 east of ORD with 85-kt jet-stream wind; FB verification report.

examples.raw: KDEN UUA /OV DEN240020 /TM 1900 /FL150 /TP C525 /TA M15 /WV 24065KT /TB SEV 120-160
examples.synoptic: Citation in mountain-wave severe turbulence west of Denver; 65-kt cross-mountain wind at FL150.
737 at FL310 east of ORD in severe turbulence; jet-stream upper-trough signature.

examples.raw: KASE UUA /OV ASE240010 /TM 1830 /FL170 /TP C525 /TA M20 /TB EXTM 150-200
examples.synoptic: Citation in extreme turbulence in Aspen mountain wave; structural-damage territory.
757 in extreme turbulence west of Denver; deep lee wave signature.
CRJ east of Aspen in extreme rotor turbulence; downstream of mountain crest.
757 reporting persistent light chop at FL360 over ORD.
737 at FL330 over Denver reporting moderate turbulence in jet-stream area.

examples.raw: KORD UA /OV ORD180010 /TM 1730 /FL060 /TP C402 /TA M02 /IC TRC RIME 040-080
examples.synoptic: C402 south of ORD with trace rime ice 4,000-8,000; non-known-ice airframe.

examples.raw: KCLT UA /OV CLT /TM 1800 /FL080 /TP PA28 /TA M03 /IC TRC RIME 070-090
examples.synoptic: PA-28 over CLT with trace rime in cool-side overrunning warm air.

examples.raw: KGRR UA /OV GRR /TM 1600 /FL050 /TP PA28 /IC LGT RIME 050-080
examples.synoptic: PA-28 over Grand Rapids with light rime ice 5,000-8,000.

examples.raw: KORD UA /OV ORD180010 /TM 1730 /FL060 /TP C402 /TA M02 /IC LGT MX 040-080
examples.synoptic: C402 south of ORD with light mixed ice 4,000-8,000; supercooled liquid + ice crystals.

examples.raw: KCLT UA /OV CLT060015 /TM 1900 /FL060 /TP PA28 /IC LGT RIME 060-080
examples.synoptic: PA-28 east of CLT in light rime icing band.

examples.raw: KGRR UUA /OV GRR /TM 1530 /FL060 /TP PA28 /TA M04 /IC MOD MX 050-070
examples.synoptic: PA-28 with moderate mixed ice 5,000-7,000; forced descent for non-known-ice airframe.

examples.raw: KCLT UUA /OV CLT270025 /TM 2030 /FL090 /TP PA32 /TA M02 /IC MOD RIME 080-110
examples.synoptic: PA-32 west of CLT in moderate rime icing band overrunning warm air.

examples.raw: KMKE UUA /OV MKE270015 /TM 1530 /FL060 /TP C402 /TA M06 /IC SEV MX 050-090
examples.synoptic: C402 west of MKE in severe mixed ice 5,000-9,000; lake-effect freezing drizzle layer.

examples.raw: KCLE UUA /OV CLE090030 /TM 1700 /FL070 /TP CRJ2 /TA M06 /IC SEV CLR 060-090
examples.synoptic: CRJ east of CLE with severe clear ice 6,000-9,000; SLD layer over Lake Erie.

tokenFamilies.label: Bulletin header
tokenFamilies.decode: FB WBC issuance source + 'DATA BASED ON' model run + 'VALID' window + 'FOR USE' range + 'TEMPS NEG ABV 24000' sign-convention banner.

references.detail: Chapter 5 - Winds and Temperatures Aloft Forecast (FB)

tokenFamilies.label: Altitude columns
tokenFamilies.decode: Standard FAA set 3000, 6000, 9000, 12000, 18000, 24000, 30000, 34000, 39000 ft MSL. Not every station reports every altitude.

references.detail: Chapter 5 - Winds and Temperatures Aloft Forecast (FB)

tokenFamilies.label: 4-char row (temp omitted)
tokenFamilies.decode: Below ~5,000 ft AGL the 3000 column drops temperature; cell is direction + speed only (4 chars).

references.detail: Chapter 5 - Winds and Temperatures Aloft Forecast (FB)

tokenFamilies.label: 6-char row (with temperature)
tokenFamilies.decode: Standard cell. Below FL240 temp carries explicit sign (+/-); at FL240 and above sign is implied negative.

references.detail: Chapter 5 - Winds and Temperatures Aloft Forecast (FB)

tokenFamilies.label: Light and variable (9900)
tokenFamilies.decode: Direction 99 + speed 00 = wind cannot be pinned (calm or weakly defined). Parser surfaces directionDeg null, speedKt 0.

references.detail: Chapter 5 - Winds and Temperatures Aloft Forecast (FB)

tokenFamilies.label: High wind (> 99 KT) - add-50 encoding
tokenFamilies.decode: When wind speed > 99 KT, FAA adds 50 to direction digit and subtracts 100 from speed. Direction values >= 50 in the encoded cell are the tell.

references.detail: Chapter 5 - Winds and Temperatures Aloft Forecast (FB)

tokenFamilies.label: Tropical / low-latitude profile
tokenFamilies.decode: Light easterlies at low altitudes (trade winds), strengthening westerlies aloft (sub-tropical jet).

references.detail: Chapter 5 - Winds and Temperatures Aloft Forecast (FB)

examples.synoptic: Boise single-station westerly profile in zonal flow; speeds climb to jet-level 90+ KT at FL390.

references.detail: Chapter 5 - Winds and Temperatures Aloft Forecast (FB)

examples.synoptic: Three-station eastern US profile showing the latitude gradient; MIA shows tropical easterlies at low levels.

references.detail: Chapter 5 - Winds and Temperatures Aloft Forecast (FB)

examples.synoptic: Denver pre-frontal col; light-and-variable at low altitudes, westerly aloft strengthening with altitude.

references.detail: Chapter 5 - Winds and Temperatures Aloft Forecast (FB)

examples.synoptic: Twin Cities entering the jet stream; FL340 and FL390 cells engage the add-50 high-wind encoding.

references.detail: Chapter 5 - Winds and Temperatures Aloft Forecast (FB)

tokenFamilies.label: AIRMET Sierra - IFR
tokenFamilies.decode: Widespread IFR (ceiling under 1,000 ft AGL or visibility under 3 SM) over more than 50% of an area. Six-hour validity.

references.detail: Chapter 7 - In-flight Weather Advisories (AIRMET / SIGMET / Convective SIGMET)
7-1-12 - Pilot Weather Reports and AIRMET / SIGMET broadcast services

tokenFamilies.label: AIRMET Sierra - mountain obscuration
tokenFamilies.decode: Clouds, precipitation, or fog obscuring mountains across an area. Six-hour validity.

references.detail: Chapter 7 - In-flight Weather Advisories (AIRMET / SIGMET / Convective SIGMET)
7-1-12 - Pilot Weather Reports and AIRMET / SIGMET broadcast services

tokenFamilies.label: AIRMET Tango - moderate turbulence
tokenFamilies.decode: Widespread moderate turbulence below FL180 (or above when not convective). Six-hour validity.

references.detail: Chapter 7 - In-flight Weather Advisories (AIRMET / SIGMET / Convective SIGMET)
7-1-12 - Pilot Weather Reports and AIRMET / SIGMET broadcast services

tokenFamilies.label: AIRMET Tango - strong surface winds
tokenFamilies.decode: Sustained surface winds > 30 KT over an area. Six-hour validity.

references.detail: Chapter 7 - In-flight Weather Advisories (AIRMET / SIGMET / Convective SIGMET)
7-1-12 - Pilot Weather Reports and AIRMET / SIGMET broadcast services

tokenFamilies.label: AIRMET Tango - low-level wind shear
tokenFamilies.decode: Non-convective LLWS below 2,000 ft AGL, typically from a low-level jet over a stable surface layer.

references.detail: Chapter 7 - In-flight Weather Advisories (AIRMET / SIGMET / Convective SIGMET)
7-1-12 - Pilot Weather Reports and AIRMET / SIGMET broadcast services

tokenFamilies.label: AIRMET Zulu - moderate icing
tokenFamilies.decode: Moderate icing between the freezing level and FL180. Six-hour validity.

references.detail: Chapter 7 - In-flight Weather Advisories (AIRMET / SIGMET / Convective SIGMET)
7-1-12 - Pilot Weather Reports and AIRMET / SIGMET broadcast services

tokenFamilies.label: AIRMET Zulu - freezing levels
tokenFamilies.decode: Forecast altitude of the 0 deg C isotherm across the AIRMET region.

references.detail: Chapter 7 - In-flight Weather Advisories (AIRMET / SIGMET / Convective SIGMET)
7-1-12 - Pilot Weather Reports and AIRMET / SIGMET broadcast services

tokenFamilies.label: SIGMET - volcanic ash
tokenFamilies.decode: Volcanic ash cloud erupted or forecast, with location and forecast motion. Four-hour validity.

references.detail: Chapter 7, non-convective SIGMET phenomena - volcanic ash
Chapter 7 - In-flight Weather Advisories (AIRMET / SIGMET / Convective SIGMET)
7-1-12 - Pilot Weather Reports and AIRMET / SIGMET broadcast services

tokenFamilies.label: SIGMET - severe turbulence (non-convective)
tokenFamilies.decode: Severe turbulence due to mountain wave, clear-air, or other non-convective driver. Four-hour validity.

references.detail: Chapter 7 - In-flight Weather Advisories (AIRMET / SIGMET / Convective SIGMET)
7-1-12 - Pilot Weather Reports and AIRMET / SIGMET broadcast services

tokenFamilies.label: SIGMET - severe icing
tokenFamilies.decode: Severe icing forecast, typically due to supercooled large droplets in a warm conveyor belt.

references.detail: Chapter 7 - In-flight Weather Advisories (AIRMET / SIGMET / Convective SIGMET)
7-1-12 - Pilot Weather Reports and AIRMET / SIGMET broadcast services

tokenFamilies.label: SIGMET - severe / widespread LLWS
tokenFamilies.decode: LLWS that exceeds the AIRMET Tango envelope. Issued by some FIRs (ZAB / ZLA / etc.) when LLJ shear is severe.

references.detail: Chapter 7 - In-flight Weather Advisories (AIRMET / SIGMET / Convective SIGMET)
7-1-12 - Pilot Weather Reports and AIRMET / SIGMET broadcast services

tokenFamilies.label: Convective SIGMET - area
tokenFamilies.decode: Cluster of cells covering >= 3,000 sq mi with >= 40% coverage. Hourly issuance at H+55, 2-hour validity.

references.detail: Chapter 7 - In-flight Weather Advisories (AIRMET / SIGMET / Convective SIGMET)
7-1-12 - Pilot Weather Reports and AIRMET / SIGMET broadcast services

tokenFamilies.label: Convective SIGMET - line
tokenFamilies.decode: Continuous line of cells >= 60 NM long with >= 40% coverage. Squall-line shape.

references.detail: Chapter 7 - In-flight Weather Advisories (AIRMET / SIGMET / Convective SIGMET)
7-1-12 - Pilot Weather Reports and AIRMET / SIGMET broadcast services

tokenFamilies.label: Convective SIGMET - embedded
tokenFamilies.decode: Cells embedded in a broader stratiform area; not visually avoidable. The IFR-flight problem.

references.detail: Chapter 7 - In-flight Weather Advisories (AIRMET / SIGMET / Convective SIGMET)
7-1-12 - Pilot Weather Reports and AIRMET / SIGMET broadcast services

tokenFamilies.label: Convective SIGMET - outlook
tokenFamilies.decode: Two-to-six-hour outlook for developing convection. Heads-up, not an active advisory.

references.detail: Chapter 7 - In-flight Weather Advisories (AIRMET / SIGMET / Convective SIGMET)
7-1-12 - Pilot Weather Reports and AIRMET / SIGMET broadcast services

examples.raw:
SFOS WA 121445
AIRMET SIERRA UPDT 2 FOR IFR VALID UNTIL 122100
AIRMET IFR... WA OR
FROM 40NNE HQM TO 50WSW SEA TO 30SSE GEG TO 30W EUG TO 40NNE HQM
CIG BLW 010 AND/OR VIS BLW 3SM PCPN/BR. CONDS CONTG BYD 21Z THRU 03Z.
examples.synoptic: Stagnant cool-season pattern over the Pacific Northwest; stratus and fog dropping ceiling under 1,000 ft over a broad area.

7-1-12 - Pilot Weather Reports and AIRMET / SIGMET broadcast services

examples.raw:

AIRMET SIERRA UPDT 2 FOR MTN OBSCN VALID UNTIL 122100
AIRMET MTN OBSCN... CA NV
FROM 40W RNO TO 40NE FAT TO 50ESE BIH TO 40SSW LAS TO 40W RNO
MTNS OBSC BY CLDS/PCPN/BR. CONDS CONTG BYD 21Z THRU 03Z.
examples.synoptic: Upslope flow against the Sierra Nevada / White Mountains hiding terrain under low cloud bases.

references.detail: Chapter 7 - In-flight Weather Advisories (AIRMET / SIGMET / Convective SIGMET)
7-1-12 - Pilot Weather Reports and AIRMET / SIGMET broadcast services

examples.raw:
SFOS WA 121445

AIRMET IFR AND MTN OBSCN... WA OR ID
FROM 40NNE HQM TO 50WSW SEA TO 30E GEG TO 30SE BOI TO 40W EUG TO 40NNE HQM
CIG BLW 010 AND/OR VIS BLW 3SM PCPN/BR. MTNS OBSC BY CLDS/PCPN/BR.
CONDS CONTG BYD 21Z THRU 03Z.
examples.synoptic: Pacific Northwest cool-season system with stratus over the lowlands and obscured Cascades and Bitterroots.

references.detail: Chapter 7 - In-flight Weather Advisories (AIRMET / SIGMET / Convective SIGMET)
7-1-12 - Pilot Weather Reports and AIRMET / SIGMET broadcast services

examples.raw:
KCIT WA 121445
AIRMET TANGO UPDT 2 FOR TURB AND STG SFC WND VALID UNTIL 122100
AIRMET TURB... CO KS NE WY
FROM 30NW BFF TO 40E LBL TO 30S ALS TO 30NW BFF
OCNL MOD TURB BTN FL240 AND FL370.
CONDS DVLPG AFT 18Z. CONDS CONTG BYD 21Z THRU 03Z.
examples.synoptic: Jet stream entrance region over the High Plains; mid-level turbulence along the upper-level jet.

references.detail: Chapter 7 - In-flight Weather Advisories (AIRMET / SIGMET / Convective SIGMET)
7-1-12 - Pilot Weather Reports and AIRMET / SIGMET broadcast services

examples.raw:
SFOT WA 121445
AIRMET TANGO UPDT 1 FOR STG SFC WND VALID UNTIL 122100
AIRMET STG SFC WND... CA
FROM 30N PRB TO 30E SBA TO 20W LAX TO 30N PRB
SUSTAINED SFC WND GTR THAN 30KT. CONDS CONTG BYD 21Z THRU 03Z.
examples.synoptic: Pacific cold front clearing southern California with strong post-frontal NW flow in the lee of the Transverse Ranges.

references.detail: Chapter 7 - In-flight Weather Advisories (AIRMET / SIGMET / Convective SIGMET)
7-1-12 - Pilot Weather Reports and AIRMET / SIGMET broadcast services

examples.raw:
KCIT WA 130845
AIRMET TANGO UPDT 1 FOR LLWS VALID UNTIL 131500
AIRMET LLWS... OK TX
FROM 30SW OKC TO 40NE ABI TO 40SW CDS TO 30SW OKC
LLWS PSBL BTN SFC AND 2000 FT AGL DUE TO LLJ.
CONDS ENDG BTN 12Z-15Z.
examples.synoptic: Nightly low-level jet over the southern Plains with > 10 KT shear in the lowest 2,000 ft.

references.detail: Chapter 7 - In-flight Weather Advisories (AIRMET / SIGMET / Convective SIGMET)
7-1-12 - Pilot Weather Reports and AIRMET / SIGMET broadcast services

examples.raw:
KCIT WA 121445
AIRMET ZULU UPDT 2 FOR ICE AND FRZLVL VALID UNTIL 122100
AIRMET ICE... IL IN OH KY WV
FROM 30W ORD TO 30E DAY TO 30S HVQ TO 30S LOZ TO 30W EVV TO 30W ORD

CONDS CONTG BYD 21Z THRU 03Z.
examples.synoptic: Warm conveyor belt over the Ohio Valley with supercooled liquid above the warm air aloft.

references.detail: Chapter 7 - In-flight Weather Advisories (AIRMET / SIGMET / Convective SIGMET)
7-1-12 - Pilot Weather Reports and AIRMET / SIGMET broadcast services

examples.raw:
KCIT WA 121445
AIRMET ZULU UPDT 2 FRZLVL... CONUS
FRZLVL RANGING FROM SFC IN N WI/MN TO 120 IN SRN TX.
MULTIPLE FRZLVLS DUE TO INVERSIONS POSS IN ID/MT/WY.
examples.synoptic: Continental freezing-level surface sloping from surface in the upper Midwest to 12,000 ft over south Texas.

references.detail: Chapter 7 - In-flight Weather Advisories (AIRMET / SIGMET / Convective SIGMET)
7-1-12 - Pilot Weather Reports and AIRMET / SIGMET broadcast services

examples.raw:
PAWU WS 130430
SIGMET INDIA 3 VALID UNTIL 130830
ANCHORAGE FIR VA ERUPTION OF MT REDOUBT VOLCANO LOC N6029 W15225
AT 0345Z. VA CLD OBS AT 0400Z LOC FROM N6030 W15230 TO N6045 W15145
TO N6115 W15030 TO N6045 W14930 SFC/FL250. MOV E 25KT.
CONDS NC.
examples.synoptic: Mt Redoubt eruption producing an ash plume drifting east through the Anchorage FIR.

examples.raw:
KKCI WS 121800
SIGMET ALFA 2 VALID UNTIL 122200
SLC FIR SEV TURB FCST WI AREA BOUNDED BY
30NE GTF TO 50E HLN TO 60SE LWT TO 40N BIL TO 30NE GTF
BTN FL280 AND FL410. DUE TO MTN WAVE.
CONDS CONTG BYD 22Z.
examples.synoptic: Strong westerly flow over the northern Rockies producing breaking mountain wave aloft.

examples.raw:
KKCI WS 121800
SIGMET LIMA 1 VALID UNTIL 122200
ZBW FIR SEV ICE FCST WI AREA BOUNDED BY
40W BUF TO 40N BDL TO 30S BDL TO 40SW BUF TO 40W BUF
BTN FL050 AND FL120. DUE TO SLW IN WARM CONVEYOR BELT.
CONDS CONTG BYD 22Z.
examples.synoptic: Warm conveyor belt over the Northeast with SLW producing severe icing in the FL050-120 layer.

examples.raw:
KKCI WS 121200
SIGMET MIKE 1 VALID UNTIL 121600
ZAB FIR SEV LLWS FCST WI AREA BOUNDED BY
40NW ABQ TO 40NE ABQ TO 30SE ABQ TO 30SW ABQ TO 40NW ABQ
BLW 020. DUE TO STG LLJ.
CONDS ENDG AFT 14Z.
examples.synoptic: Strong overnight LLJ over central New Mexico producing severe shear below 2,000 ft AGL.

examples.raw:
MKCC WST 121955
CONVECTIVE SIGMET 23C VALID UNTIL 122155
KS NE OK
FROM 30NW ICT-30S TOP-50E AMA
AREA TS MOV FROM 24025KT. TOPS TO FL450. HAIL TO 1 IN PSBL. WIND GUSTS TO 50KT PSBL.
examples.synoptic: Cluster of thunderstorms over the southern Plains moving NE in the warm sector ahead of a cold front.

TX OK
FROM 20NW SPS-30E ADM-20S ADM
LINE TS 50NM WIDE MOV FROM 26035KT. TOPS TO FL480. HAIL TO 2 IN PSBL.

examples.raw:
MKCC WST 121955
CONVECTIVE SIGMET 1 OUTLOOK VALID 122200-130200
FROM 50NW DSM-30E DSM-30E STL-50W STL
WST ISSUANCES PSBL. REF AC. AREAS OF TS DVLPG IN ADVANCE OF FRONT.

examples.raw:
KCIT WA 121445
AIRMET SIERRA UPDT 2 FOR IFR VALID UNTIL 122100
AIRMET IFR... TX OK KS
FROM 30NW DFW TO 30SE OKC TO 50S ICT TO 60SW ABI TO 30NW DFW
CIG BLW 010 AND/OR VIS BLW 3SM PCPN/BR.
examples.synoptic: Springtime cold front through the southern Plains with broad pre-frontal IFR and post-frontal convection.

references.detail: Chapter 7 - In-flight Weather Advisories (AIRMET / SIGMET / Convective SIGMET)
7-1-12 - Pilot Weather Reports and AIRMET / SIGMET broadcast services

examples.raw:
SFOZ WA 121445
AIRMET ZULU UPDT 1 FOR ICE AND FRZLVL VALID UNTIL 122100
AIRMET ICE... WA OR
FROM 40NNE HQM TO 50E SEA TO 30E PDX TO 40W EUG TO 40NNE HQM
MOD ICE BTN FRZLVL AND 100. FRZLVL SFC TO 040.
LCL SEV ICE PSBL IN SLW DUE TO FRZ DRIZZLE.
CONDS CONTG BYD 21Z THRU 03Z.
examples.synoptic: Stagnant Pacific Northwest pattern with stratus, embedded freezing drizzle, and surface-skimming freezing level.

examples.raw:
PAWU WS 130430
SIGMET INDIA 3 VALID UNTIL 130830
ANCHORAGE FIR VA ERUPTION OF MT REDOUBT VOLCANO LOC N6029 W15225
